title: Multi-Task Loss Functions
concept: Mask R-CNN uses a multi-task loss function that combines classification, bounding box localization, and pixel-level mask losses.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the benefit of using sigmoid instead of softmax in the mask branch?

options.text: It decouples mask prediction from class prediction, avoiding class competition
It speeds up inference on edge devices by using integers
It eliminates the need for bounding boxes

exercises.explain: Correct! Sigmoid allows independent pixel prediction per class.

cards.title: Balancing the grades
cards.body: Mask R-CNN does three things at once: classifies, draws boxes, and paints masks. When training, the model makes mistakes in all three. How do we grade a model that has three different homework assignments? We combine all three grades into a single, multi-task loss score.

cards.title: Multi-Task Loss
cards.body: The total loss of Mask R-CNN is defined as: $$L = L_{cls} + L_{box} + L_{mask}$$ where $L_{cls}$ is classification loss, $L_{box}$ is bounding box loss, and $L_{mask}$ is the average binary cross-entropy loss for the mask branch.

cards.title: Three-legged race
cards.body: Imagine a team in a three-legged race. To win, all members must move together. The multi-task loss is like a score that penalizes the team if the runner, the jumper, or the navigator falls behind, keeping them aligned.

cards.title: Loss calculation
cards.body: During training, if the model predicts the correct class ('dog') and box, but gets the tail mask wrong, $L_{cls}$ and $L_{box}$ will be low, but $L_{mask}$ will be high, forcing the optimizer to adjust the mask weights.

cards.title: Only the ground truth mask counts
cards.body: You might think we calculate mask loss for all $K$ classes. We don't! $L_{mask}$ is only calculated on the mask corresponding to the **ground truth class**. The other class masks are ignored during that step to avoid confusing the network.

left.text:
Classification & Box Regression
Identical to Faster R-CNN loss

right.text:
Binary Cross-Entropy per pixel
Only computed for the true class mask

exercises.prompt: What is the formula for the multi-task loss in Mask R-CNN?
exercises.explain: Correct! The losses are summed together to optimize all tasks simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Mask loss is calculated for all classes during every forward pass.
exercises.explain: False. Mask loss is only computed for the ground truth class mask.

exercises.sentence: The mask loss component uses binary ___ entropy for each pixel.
exercises.explain: Binary cross-entropy is used to classify each pixel as foreground or background.

exercises.prompt: What does a high L_mask value indicate during training?

options.text: The predicted pixel mask is not aligned with the true mask contour
The bounding box coordinates are completely wrong
The class label prediction is incorrect

exercises.explain: A high mask loss means the pixel-level mask needs refinement.

exercises.prompt: If a Mask R-CNN model predicts the correct bounding box and class label, but the mask loss is extremely high, how will the weights update?

options.text: The optimizer will primarily update the weights of the mask branch, as the classification and box weights are already producing low loss.
The entire model will freeze and stop updating.
The optimizer will increase the learning rate of the backbone only.
The box regression branch weights will be reset to zero.

exercises.explain: Exactly! The multi-task loss allows different parts of the network to focus on their respective errors, updating the mask branch when masks are bad. Next: let's see Mask R-CNN in practice!
